similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths about the obligation to return the greeting of salam and the etiquette of greeting in Islam, including the merit of spreading peace among believers.
Narrations teaching that spreading salam is a sign of love among Muslims and one of the rights a Muslim has upon another Muslim.
Traditions encouraging humility in social interactions, where the one who humbles themselves first by greeting others is rewarded with divine closeness.

summaries.bn: ইমাম সাদিক (আ.) শিক্ষা দিয়েছেন যে যে ব্যক্তি প্রথমে সালাম দেওয়ার উদ্যোগ নেয়, সে আল্লাহ ও তাঁর রাসূলের অধিক নিকটতম। এই হাদীস সালামে অগ্রণী হওয়ার ফজিলত এবং ইসলামী সামাজিক সৌহার্দ্য প্রতিষ্ঠায় সালামের ভূমিকা তুলে ধরে।
summaries.de: Imam al-Sadiq (F) lehrt, dass derjenige, der die Initiative ergreift und den islamischen Friedensgruß (Salam) zuerst gibt, Gott und dem Propheten am nächsten steht. Dieser Hadith ermutigt Muslime, im Gruß proaktiv zu sein, da derjenige, der zuerst grüßt, größere Tugend, Demut und geistige Großzügigkeit zeigt.
summaries.en: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) teaches that the person who takes the initiative to give the Islamic greeting of peace (salam) holds a higher standing with God and the Prophet. This hadith encourages Muslims to be proactive in spreading peace and goodwill, as the one who greets first demonstrates the greater virtue of social warmth and humility. The 'closeness to God and His Messenger' here conveys superior moral rank and divine favor.
summaries.es: El Imam al-Sadiq (P) enseña que quien toma la iniciativa de dar el saludo islámico de la paz (salam) tiene un mayor rango espiritual ante Dios y el Profeta. Este hadith exhorta a los musulmanes a ser proactivos en extender la paz y la cordialidad, pues el que saluda primero demuestra mayor virtud, humildad y generosidad espiritual.
summaries.fa: امام صادق علیه السلام فرمودند که آغازگر سلام به خدا و رسولش نزدیک‌تر است. این حدیث فضیلت پیشقدمی در سلام و نقش آن در ایجاد محبت و برادری اسلامی را بیان می‌کند. سلام دادن سنت پیامبر صلی الله علیه وآله است.
summaries.fr: L'Imam al-Sadiq (P) enseigne que celui qui prend l'initiative de donner le salut islamique de paix (salam) est plus proche de Dieu et de Son Prophète. Ce hadith encourage les musulmans à être proactifs dans la propagation de la paix, car celui qui salue en premier fait preuve d'une plus grande vertu, humilité et générosité spirituelle.
summaries.id: Imam Shadiq (a.s.) mengajarkan bahwa orang yang berinisiatif mengucapkan salam lebih dekat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya. Hadits ini mendorong umat Islam untuk proaktif dalam menyebarkan salam sebagai bentuk kerendahan hati dan kasih sayang. Menyebarkan salam adalah sunnah yang mempererat ukhuwah islamiyah.
summaries.ru: Имам ас-Садик (мир ему) учит, что тот, кто берёт инициативу и первым произносит исламское приветствие мира (салам), занимает более высокое место перед Аллахом и Пророком. Этот хадис поощряет мусульман проявлять инициативу в распространении мира, поскольку тот, кто приветствует первым, демонстрирует большую добродетель, смирение и духовную щедрость.
summaries.tr: İmam Sadık (a.s.), selamı veren kimsenin Allah ve Peygamber'e daha yakın olduğunu buyurmuştur. Bu hadis, selamlamada önce davrananın üstün bir erdem sergilediğini ve ilahi yakınlık kazandığını öğretmektedir. Selamı yaymak İslami toplumsallığın temelidir.
summaries.ur: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے فرمایا کہ جو شخص پہلے سلام کرے وہ اللہ اور اس کے رسول کے نزدیک تر ہے۔ یہ حدیث سلام میں پہل کرنے کی فضیلت اور اس کے ذریعے اللہ کی قربت حاصل کرنے کی تعلیم دیتی ہے۔ سلام پھیلانا اسلامی معاشرت کی بنیاد ہے۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）教导说，主动先行问安（萨拉姆）的人在真主和先知那里享有更高的地位。这段圣训鼓励穆斯林主动传播和平与友爱，因为先问候者展现了更大的美德、谦逊和精神慷慨。问候是伊斯兰社群纽带的重要基础。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/541)
text: الْبَادِي بِالسَّلَامِ أَوْلَى بِاللَّهِ وَ بِرَسُولِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Isa, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The initiator with the greeting (السَّلَامُ) is the closest of the people with Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Mahbub from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The initiator of the offering of the greeting of peace is closer to Allah and His Messenger.”

translations.bn: আমি আবু আবদিল্লাহ (আলাইহিস সালাম)-কে বলতে শুনেছি: যে «আস-সালামু আলাইকুম» বলে তার দশটি নেকী, যে «আস-সালামু আলাইকুম ওয়া রাহমাতুল্লাহ» বলে তার বিশটি নেকী, এবং যে «আস-সালামু আলাইকুম ওয়া রাহমাতুল্লাহি ওয়া বারাকাতুহু» বলে তার ত্রিশটি নেকী হবে।
translations.de: Ich hörte Abu Abdillah (a.s.) sagen: Wer «as-Salamu alaykum» sagt, das sind zehn gute Taten; wer «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullah» sagt, das sind zwanzig gute Taten; und wer «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh» sagt, das sind dreißig gute Taten.
translations.en: I heard Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) saying: 'Whoever says "Peace be upon you" (al-Salamu alaykum), it is ten good deeds. And whoever says "Peace be upon you and the mercy of God" (al-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullah), it is twenty good deeds. And whoever says "Peace be upon you and the mercy of God and His blessings" (al-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh), it is thirty good deeds.'
translations.es: Escuché a Abu Abdillah (a.s.) decir: Quien dice «as-Salamu alaykum» son diez buenas obras, quien dice «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullah» son veinte buenas obras, y quien dice «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh» son treinta buenas obras.
translations.fa: شنیدم ابو عبدالله علیه السلام می‌فرمود: هر کس «السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ» بگوید ده حسنه، هر کس «السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ» بگوید بیست حسنه، و هر کس «السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ» بگوید سی حسنه خواهد بود.
translations.fr: J'ai entendu Abu Abdillah (a.s.) dire : Quiconque dit «as-Salamu alaykum» ce sont dix bonnes actions, quiconque dit «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullah» ce sont vingt bonnes actions, et quiconque dit «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh» ce sont trente bonnes actions.
translations.id: Aku mendengar Abu Abdillah (a.s.) bersabda: Barangsiapa mengucapkan «as-Salamu alaykum» maka itu sepuluh kebaikan, barangsiapa mengucapkan «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullah» maka itu dua puluh kebaikan, dan barangsiapa mengucapkan «as-Salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh» maka itu tiga puluh kebaikan.
translations.ru: Я слышал, как Абу Абдиллах (мир ему) говорил: Кто скажет «ас-Саламу алейкум» — это десять добрых дел; кто скажет «ас-Саламу алейкум ва рахматуллах» — это двадцать добрых дел; а кто скажет «ас-Саламу алейкум ва рахматуллахи ва баракатух» — это тридцать добрых дел.
translations.tr: Ebu Abdullah (a.s.)'ı şöyle buyururken duydum: Kim «es-Selâmü aleyküm» derse on iyilik, kim «es-Selâmü aleyküm ve rahmetüllah» derse yirmi iyilik, kim «es-Selâmü aleyküm ve rahmetüllahi ve berekâtüh» derse otuz iyiliktir.
translations.ur: میں نے ابو عبد اللہ علیہ السلام کو فرماتے ہوئے سنا: جو «السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ» کہے اسے دس نیکیاں ملتی ہیں، جو «السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ» کہے اسے بیس نیکیاں ملتی ہیں، اور جو «السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ» کہے اسے تیس نیکیاں ملتی ہیں۔
translations.zh: 我听到阿布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）说道：凡说「安赛俩目尔来库姆」者，那就是十件善功；凡说「安赛俩目尔来库姆·瓦·拉赫麦图拉」者，那就是二十件善功；凡说「安赛俩目尔来库姆·瓦·拉赫麦图拉希·瓦·巴拉卡图胡」者，那就是三十件善功。

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الْمُنْذِرِ قَالَ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Ali ibn al-Hakam al-Ansari, a prolific transmitter of Shia hadith from the era of Imams al-Sadiq and al-Kazim.

narrators.known_identity: Aban ibn Uthman al-Ahmar (likely)

seo_questions.bn: ইসলামে আস-সালামু আলাইকুম ওয়া রাহমাতুল্লাহি ওয়া বারাকাতুহু বলার কত নেকী?
seo_questions.de: Wie viele gute Taten bringt es, im Islam as-salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh zu sagen?
seo_questions.en: How many good deeds does saying as-salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh earn in Islam?
seo_questions.es: ¿Cuántas buenas obras se obtienen al decir as-salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh en el Islam?
seo_questions.fa: گفتن السلام علیکم و رحمة الله و برکاته چند حسنه دارد؟
seo_questions.fr: Combien de bonnes actions vaut dire as-salamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh en Islam ?
seo_questions.id: Berapa pahala mengucapkan assalamu alaikum warahmatullahi wabarakatuh dalam Islam?
seo_questions.ru: Сколько добрых дел приносит произнесение ас-саламу алейкум ва рахматуллахи ва баракатух в исламе?
seo_questions.tr: İslam'da es-Selâmü aleyküm ve rahmetüllahi ve berekâtüh demek kaç sevap kazandırır?
seo_questions.ur: السلام علیکم ورحمۃ اللہ وبرکاتہ کہنے پر کتنی نیکیاں ملتی ہیں؟
seo_questions.zh: 在伊斯兰教中，念安赛俩目尔来库姆瓦拉赫麦图拉希瓦巴拉卡图胡能得多少善功？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths about the obligation and reward of initiating the Islamic greeting (salam), including who should greet whom first (the rider greets the pedestrian, the small group greets the large, etc.).
Quranic verses and hadiths about responding to a greeting with an equal or better greeting, referencing the duty of reply in Surah al-Nisa (4:86).
Hadiths enumerating the spiritual rewards (hasanat) for common religious phrases and dhikr, such as tasbih, tahmid, and takbir, showing the theme of incremental reward for more complete formulas.

summaries.bn: ইমাম জাফর সাদিক (আলাইহিস সালাম) শিক্ষা দেন যে ইসলামিক সালামের আধ্যাত্মিক মূল্য তার পূর্ণতার অনুপাতে বাড়ে। সাধারণ সালাম দশটি নেকী, 'ওয়া রাহমাতুল্লাহ' যোগ করলে বিশটি এবং পূর্ণ সালামে ত্রিশটি নেকী পাওয়া যায়।
summaries.de: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (Friede sei mit ihm) lehrt, dass der spirituelle Wert des islamischen Grußes proportional zu seiner Vollständigkeit ist. Der grundlegende Gruß bringt zehn gute Taten, das Hinzufügen von 'wa rahmatullah' verdoppelt ihn auf zwanzig, und der vollständige Gruß mit 'wa barakatuh' bringt dreißig gute Taten.
summaries.en: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) teaches that the Islamic greeting carries spiritual merit proportional to its completeness. The basic greeting earns ten good deeds, adding 'wa rahmatullah' doubles this to twenty, and the full greeting with 'wa barakatuh' earns thirty good deeds. This teaching reflects the Islamic emphasis on greeting as an act of worship that cultivates brotherhood and divine reward.
summaries.es: El Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (la paz sea con él) enseña que el valor espiritual del saludo islámico es proporcional a su completitud. El saludo básico otorga diez buenas obras, añadir 'wa rahmatullah' lo duplica a veinte, y el saludo completo con 'wa barakatuh' otorga treinta buenas obras.
summaries.fa: امام جعفر صادق علیه السلام می‌آموزند که ارزش روحانی سلام اسلامی با کمال آن نسبت مستقیم دارد. سلام ساده ده حسنه، افزودن «و رحمة الله» بیست حسنه و سلام کامل با «و برکاته» سی حسنه در پی دارد.
summaries.fr: L'Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (que la paix soit sur lui) enseigne que la valeur spirituelle du salut islamique est proportionnelle à sa complétude. Le salut de base vaut dix bonnes actions, l'ajout de 'wa rahmatullah' le double à vingt, et le salut complet avec 'wa barakatuh' vaut trente bonnes actions.
summaries.id: Imam Ja'far al-Shadiq (a.s.) mengajarkan bahwa nilai spiritual salam Islam sebanding dengan kelengkapannya. Salam dasar mendatangkan sepuluh kebaikan, menambahkan 'wa rahmatullah' menjadi dua puluh, dan salam lengkap dengan 'wa barakatuh' mendatangkan tiga puluh kebaikan.
summaries.ru: Имам Джафар ас-Садик (мир ему) учит, что духовная ценность исламского приветствия пропорциональна его полноте. Простое приветствие приносит десять добрых дел, добавление «ва рахматуллах» — двадцать, а полное приветствие с «ва баракатух» — тридцать добрых дел.
summaries.tr: İmam Cafer es-Sadık (a.s.), İslam selamının manevi değerinin tamlığıyla orantılı olduğunu öğretmektedir. Temel selam on iyilik, 'rahmetullah' eklenmesiyle yirmi iyilik ve 'berekâtüh' ile tam selam otuz iyilik kazandırır.
summaries.ur: امام جعفر صادق علیہ السلام نے بیان فرمایا کہ اسلامی سلام کا ثواب اس کی مکملیت کے ساتھ بڑھتا ہے۔ سادہ سلام دس نیکیوں کا موجب ہے، رحمت کا اضافہ بیس نیکیاں دلاتا ہے اور برکات کے ساتھ مکمل سلام تیس نیکیاں عطا کرتا ہے۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目贾法尔·萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）教导说，伊斯兰问候语的精神价值与其完整程度成正比。简单的问候可得十件善功，加上「瓦·拉赫麦图拉」可得二十件，而加上「瓦·巴拉卡图胡」的完整问候则可得三十件善功。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/541)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَنْ قَالَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَهِيَ عَشْرُ حَسَنَاتٍ وَ مَنْ قَالَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ فَهِيَ عِشْرُونَ حَسَنَةً وَ مَنْ قَالَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَ بَرَكَاتُهُ فَهِيَ ثَلَاثُونَ حَسَنَةً .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Aban, from Al hassan Bin Al Munzir who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The one who says ‘The greetings be upon you’ (السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ), so it would be ten good deeds, and the one who says, ‘The greetings be upon you and the Mercy of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’ (السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ), so it is twenty good deeds, and the one who says, ‘The greetings be upon you, and the Mercy of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and His<sup>azwj</sup> Blessings’ (السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَ بَرَكَاتُهُ), so it is thirty good deeds’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Aban from al-Hassan ibn al-Mundhir who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) saying, ‘If one offering the greeting of peace says, “(I offer) you to be in peace,” it is equal to ten good deeds. Saying, ‘(I offer) you to be in peace, and May Allah grant you favors,’ is equal to twenty good deeds. Saying, ‘(I offer) you to be in peace, and May Allah grant you favors and blessings,’ is equal to thirty good deeds.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/541)
text: ثَلَاثَةٌ تُرَدُّ عَلَيْهِمْ رَدَّ الْجَمَاعَةِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ وَاحِداً عِنْدَ الْعُطَاسِ يُقَالُ يَرْحَمُكُمُ اللَّهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ مَعَهُ غَيْرُهُ وَ الرَّجُلُ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ فَيَقُولُ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَ الرَّجُلُ يَدْعُو لِلرَّجُلِ فَيَقُولُ عَافَاكُمُ اللَّهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ وَاحِداً فَإِنَّ مَعَهُ غَيْرَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ja’far Bin Bashir, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Three would be responded upon by the plural response, and even though it may be one (person only) – during the sneeze it is said, ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> have Mercy on you all’, and even if there does not happen to be someone else with him; and the man greets upon the man, so he is saying, ‘The greetings be upon you all’, and the man supplicating for the man, so he is saying, ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Grant good health to you all’, and even if he is alone; for there are others (Angels) with him’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Salih ibn al-Sindi from Ja’far ibn Bashir from Mansur ibn Hazim from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “In three cases the ‘greeting’ is answered in a plural case even if there is only one person: for one’s sneezing, it is said, ‘May Allah grant you (plural) favor.’ when a man offers the ‘greeting of peace’ saying, ‘(I offer you to) be in peace’, the answer is ‘(I offer you to) be in peace,’ and when a man pleads before Allah for good health for another man, he should say, ‘May Allah grant you (plural) good health, even if there is only one person; there are others with him (the guarding angels).”’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/541)
text: كَانَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ ثَلَاثَةٌ لَا يُسَلَّمُونَ الْمَاشِي مَعَ الْجَنَازَةِ وَ الْمَاشِي إِلَى الْجُمُعَةِ وَ فِي بَيْتِ الْحَمَّامِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, raising it, said,
‘It was so that Abu Abdullahasws was saying: ‘Three (persons) would not be greeting (السَّلَامُ) – the walker along with the funeral, and the walker to the Friday Salāt, and in the bath house’.
en.sarwar: 11. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn in a marfu’ manner from an Imam who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) would say, ‘Three kinds of people do not offer greetings: one walking in funeral procession, one walking to attend Friday prayer and in the bathing places.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/542)
text: مِنَ التَّوَاضُعِ أَنْ تُسَلِّمَ عَلَى مَنْ لَقِيتَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Haroun Bin Kharija,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘(It is) from the reverence that you greet (السَّلَامُ) upon the one you meet’.
en.sarwar: 12. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Harun ibn Kharijah from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “It is of humility to offer the greeting of peace to whomever you meet.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/542)
text: مَرَّ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) بِقَوْمٍ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِمْ فَقَالُوا عَلَيْكَ السَّلَامُ وَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَ بَرَكَاتُهُ وَ مَغْفِرَتُهُ وَ رِضْوَانُهُ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) لَا تُجَاوِزُوا بِنَا مِثْلَ مَا قَالَتِ الْمَلَائِكَةُ لِأَبِينَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّمَا قَالُوا رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَ بَرَكَاتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Jameel, from Abu Ubeyda Al Haza’a,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen Ali<sup>asws</sup> passed by a group of people, so he<sup>asws</sup> greeted (السَّلَامُ) upon them. So they said, ‘Upon you<sup>asws</sup> be the greetings, and Mercy of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and His<sup>azwj</sup> Blessings, and His<sup>azwj</sup> Pleasure’. So Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said to them: ‘Do not say to us<sup>asws</sup> more than what the Angels said to our<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>as</sup> Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>. But rather, they said, ‘Mercy of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Blessings be upon you<sup>as</sup>, the People of the Household’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn Mahbub from Jamil from abu ‘Ubaydah al-Hadhdha’ from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) passed by a people and offered them the greeting of peace and they answered, ‘(We offer) you peace, May Allah grant you favors, blessings, forgiveness and His pleasure.’ Amir al-Mu’Minin (a.s.) said to them, ‘Do not say to us more than what the angels said to Ibrahim. They said, ‘May Allah grant you favors and blessings, O the people of the house’ (of the Holy Prophet).”’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: سكت عنه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/542)
text: إِنَّ مِنْ تَمَامِ التَّحِيَّةِ لِلْمُقِيمِ الْمُصَافَحَةَ وَ تَمَامِ التَّسْلِيمِ عَلَى الْمُسَافِرِ الْمُعَانَقَةَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali Bin Raib,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘From the complete welcome for the staying one (not travelling), is the handshake, and the complete greeting (السَّلَامُ) upon the traveller is the hugging’.
en.sarwar: 14. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn Ri’ab from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The completion of greetings of peace for one at home is to shake hands and for one on a journey it is to hold him in one’s arms.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: سكت عنه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/542)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) يُكْرَهُ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَقُولَ حَيَّاكَ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ يَسْكُتَ حَتَّى يَتْبَعَهَا بِالسَّلَامِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> disliked it for the man that he should be saying, ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Keep you alive’, then he is silent, without offering the greetings of peace’.
en.sarwar: 15. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfali from al-Sakuni from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) has said, ‘It is undesirable for a man to say, “May Allah keep you living,” then remain silent without offering the greeting of peace thereafter.’”
